title: Iron Snake Level Transition Board Morph Animation (JOE-5)
content:
## Overview

When a player completes a level in Iron Snake mode, the board does not swap instantly. Instead, the playable region **morphs cell-by-cell from the old shape into the new one**, driven at the game's own move cadence. The snake never relocates — the new board is grown around the snake's current cells.

## Behavior

- The board outline, floor tiles, and grid lines all reshape together during the transition because all three drawing functions (`drawGroundPlane`, `drawGridLines`, `drawGroundBorder`) read a single `boardMask: Uint8Array | null` field. Feeding the renderer an incrementally-mutated display mask each frame produces the cell-by-cell geometric effect with no changes to any rendering code.
- Changed cells are revealed in **inside-out wave order** — sorted by distance from the board center ascending — so the arena appears to grow outward and old-only cells peel away as the wave passes.
- Each reveal step is held for approximately one snake-move duration, so the reshape unfolds at the game's own rhythm rather than a fixed wall-clock duration.
- The snake and food are drawn on top of the mask as usual; snake cells are grounded into the start display mask so nothing floats over void during the transition.
- The morph **always plays** — the earlier `prefers-reduced-motion` gate was removed after it caused the transition to be invisible on machines with that OS setting enabled.

## Board Generation: `growIronSnakeBoard`

The new board is generated by `growIronSnakeBoard` in `src/iron-snake.ts`. It:

1. Seeds a clear room around the snake's combined bounding box (not the old board).
2. Grows fresh overlapping rooms outward until the board reaches target fill.

This guarantees a visibly different shape every level regardless of whether the grid size increases. The previous approach embedded the old board into the new grid and added rooms until target fill — when the grid size stayed the same (floored at size 12 at early levels), the old board was already at target fill and zero new rooms were added, producing an identical shape.

## Snake Placement: `applyLevelAdvance`

`applyLevelAdvance` in `src/main.ts` replaces the old `reseatKeepingShape` pipeline. It:

- Keeps both snakes exactly in place (grid coordinates are preserved across the level boundary).
- Calls `growIronSnakeBoard` with the current snake cells so the board is guaranteed to include every cell the snake occupies by construction.
- When the grid size grows between levels, calls `translateSnakes` to shift both snakes by the centering offset so they remain visually anchored on screen.

The entire shape-rotation reseat chain — `placeRigid`, `layoutBody`, `placeSnakeKeepingShape`, `rotateOffset`, `rotateDirection`, `reseatKeepingShape` — was deleted as dead code. `vectorToDirection` was retained as it is still used by tail-direction rendering logic.

## Morph Animation: `startLevelMorph` / `morphStep` / `finishLevelMorph` / `cancelLevelMorph`

All morph logic lives in `src/main.ts`.

**State fields and tuning constants:**
- `isTransitioning: boolean` — suppresses normal per-tick repaints while a morph is in progress.
- `transitionRafId: number | null` — handle for the in-flight `requestAnimationFrame` loop.
- `LEVEL_MORPH_MIN_STEPS`, `LEVEL_MORPH_MAX_STEPS`, `LEVEL_MORPH_CELLS_PER_STEP`, `LEVEL_MORPH_MIN_STEP_MS` — tuning constants controlling pacing.

**Morph lifecycle:**
1. `startLevelMorph` builds a `startDisplay` mask (old board centered in the new grid, with snake and food cells unioned in) and a `targetDisplay` mask, collects the differing cells sorted as an inside-out wave, tears down the logic `setInterval`, sets `isTransitioning = true`, and starts the `requestAnimationFrame` loop.
2. `morphStep` runs each frame: reveals the next batch of changed cells into a working buffer, assigns `this.boardMask = workBuffer`, and calls `draw()`.
3. `finishLevelMorph` restores `this.boardMask` to the real target value, calls `draw()` once cleanly, restarts the logic interval at the new `gameSpeed`, and clears transition flags.
4. `cancelLevelMorph` is called from `startNewGame` and both back-to-menu handlers to cancel any in-flight morph and clean up state.

**Guards:**
- `updateSinglePlayer` and `updateTwoPlayer` skip their normal trailing `draw()` call while `isTransitioning` is true.
- The logic interval is torn down at morph start and restarted in `finishLevelMorph`.

## Files

- `src/iron-snake.ts` — `growIronSnakeBoard`
- `src/main.ts` — `applyLevelAdvance`, `startLevelMorph`, `morphStep`, `finishLevelMorph`, `cancelLevelMorph`, morph state fields and tuning constants, draw guards
